summary: Add the FR-15.1 desktop disk-cache slice: strict binary-safe records with versioned SHA-256 integrity, raw-size and IPC caps, native transactional LRU eviction, per-project opaque consent persistence, cold-start lookup, explicit clear action, Tauri app-cache commands, and honest privacy/user-guide disclosure.

decisions.decision: Keep disk-cache bytes behind a platform-owned storage interface keyed by project identity and cryptographic render key; the application cache implementation never receives a project path and never writes source files.
decisions.rationale: Q-0036 permits only an opt-in per-project tier under the application cache directory; a storage seam lets the desktop adapter enforce that placement without coupling application code to Tauri or filesystem APIs.

decisions.decision: Persist a versioned envelope containing project identity and exact key, encode 3D bytes as base64, reject malformed or mismatched records as misses, remove parse-corrupt records, and clone decoded results before returning them.
decisions.rationale: Disk bytes survive crashes and upgrades; explicit identity binding and immutable handoff prevent stale/cross-project reuse and viewer mutation.

decisions.topic: Desktop IPC safety and LRU metadata
decisions.decision: Keep the disk tier's nominal 512 MiB project budget but cap each persisted JSON/IPC record at 4 MiB, leaving larger geometry memory-only. Native storage rejects oversized files before reading payload bytes, and access recency lives in a small sidecar updated under the same process mutex rather than rewriting geometry on every hit.
decisions.rationale: Tauri nested values are JSON-serialized rather than raw binary; a bounded record cap keeps transient IPC allocations safe while preserving the memory tier. Sidecar metadata keeps cached display and eviction independent of multi-megabyte payload rewrites.

decisions.decision: Persist disk-cache consent independently by opaque desktop workspace identity; keep scratch work ineligible, exclude consent from portable settings, and expose an active-project clear action.
decisions.rationale: FR-15.1 requires an opt-in per-project desktop tier. A global rendering preference would silently enable unrelated projects and would be misleading in settings exports.

decisions.decision: Wrap each record in a versioned integrity envelope whose SHA-256 digest covers the exact scoped payload; reject and remove missing, malformed, mismatched, or tampered records as cache misses.
decisions.rationale: Durable cache bytes can be truncated or modified across crashes and upgrades; structural decoding alone cannot detect valid-shape corruption.

decisions.decision: Pass the configured budget to the native Tauri write command and enforce cleanup, deterministic LRU eviction, and atomic replacement under the native cache mutex.
decisions.rationale: Separate list/remove/write calls can race across runtime instances; a single native transaction keeps record plus sidecar bytes within the configured budget and cleans crash artifacts.

decisions.decision: Document durable geometry, logs, diagnostics, statistics, integrity/LRU metadata, default-off consent, disable-without-delete behavior, and the per-project clear control in README, PRIVACY.md, and the user guide.
decisions.rationale: N-6 and N-7 require honest point-of-use labeling and complete local-retention disclosure for cached or simulated data.

testEvidence.criterion: Disk records round-trip binary geometry, report disk tier, and isolate returned mutations.
testEvidence.test: Run the dedicated RenderDiskCache unit suite.

red.outcome: RED: the first test expected one access touch but the implementation correctly touched on both cache reads; the assertion was corrected.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 11 passed (11), including raw SVG/log preflight, digest tamper rejection, clear quiescing, and native-budget forwarding.

testEvidence.criterion: The disk model is type-safe and lint/source-policy clean.
testEvidence.test: Run typecheck and lint once after the final import correction.

red.outcome: RED: decoded bounding-box tuple values were still typed as unknown.

green.outcome: GREEN: tsc --noEmit exited 0; Biome checked 402 files with no fixes; source policy passed.

testEvidence.criterion: The tiered runtime, per-project consent/clear UI capability gate, Tauri adapter, dependency-closure key, and disk model work together on the exact current tree.
testEvidence.test: Run the eight-file scoped application/platform/UI suite once.

red.outcome: RED: the initial global-settings design failed the per-project consent and clear-lifecycle falsification cases.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 8 passed (8); Tests 110 passed (110); Duration 2.69s, including nested include/use, unresolved/external dependency refusal, dynamic/conditional-import keys, a 64-file application hashing benchmark under 100ms, per-project consent, restart, clear-isolation, Tauri adapter, and UI coverage.

testEvidence.criterion: The native Tauri cache commands compile and their Rust unit suite remains green.
testEvidence.test: Run cargo fmt check and offline Rust tests once.

red.outcome: RED baseline unavailable: this command was not run against the final tree; no product failure is claimed.

green.command: cargo fmt --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --check; cargo test --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --offline
green.outcome: GREEN: formatting passed; 41 Rust tests passed with 0 failures, including bounded native transaction, stale-artifact cleanup, and clear-command coverage.

testEvidence.criterion: The current production web bundle includes the desktop-cache settings/UI changes without build errors.

red.outcome: RED baseline unavailable: this command was not run against the final tree; no product failure is claimed.

green.outcome: GREEN: Vite transformed 220 modules and built the production bundle successfully in 342ms.

nearMisses.sought: A deterministic native subprocess latency measurement for the cold disk-cache lookup.
nearMisses.confirmation: The application evidence measures 64-file dependency hashing under 100ms with a mocked cache; the native engine probe, disk IPC, and clean-host timing remain unmeasured and are intentionally deferred to the Windows evidence gate.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
